question: Is the central figure clearly recognizable as the brown teddy bear with a red bow tie, matching the subject reference (Image 1)?
Does the teddy bear retain its plush, furry texture and specific facial features (round ears, snout) despite the lighting changes?
Does the image adopt the cold, dark color palette (teal, cyan, deep blues, and blacks) of the horror style reference (Image 2)?
Is the lighting simulated as a harsh, directional flashlight beam that creates a bright spotlight on the subject against darkness?
Does the image exhibit visible film grain, noise, or lens flare effects consistent with the visual texture of the horror reference?
Does the environment strictly follow the geometry of the concentric square spiral corridor shown in the depth map (Image 3)?
Is the teddy bear positioned at the far end (vanishing point) of the corridor, consistent with the depth map's layout?
Is the teddy bear scaled appropriately small to reflect its distance at the end of the deep perspective corridor?
Does the teddy bear blend naturally into the scene with appropriate shadows and lighting interaction, avoiding a 'sticker' look?
Is the image free from significant anatomical distortions (e.g., melted limbs) or geometric breaks in the corridor structure?
